title: Is It Too Late To Consider IAMGOLD (TSX:IMG) After 176% One-Year Surge?
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

Considering whether IAMGOLD's share price reflects its true worth, or if the market might be mispricing it right now. At a last close of C$27.69, the stock has returns of 4.1% over 7 days, 1.7% over 30 days, 24.5% year to date, 176.3% over 1 year, and very large gains over 3 and 5 years. Recent coverage has focused on IAMGOLD's position in the gold sector and the way investor sentiment has shifted around producers with meaningful leverage to the gold price. This context helps explain why the share price has seen strong longer term returns, alongside shorter term moves that are more modest. With a valuation score of 6 out of 6, IAMGOLD screens as undervalued on each of the checks used here. The next sections will walk through the standard valuation approaches, then finish with a way of thinking about value that can give an even clearer picture.

IAMGOLD delivered 176.3% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Metals and Mining industry.

Approach 1: IAMGOLD Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting the cash it may generate in the future and then discounting those amounts back to today.

For IAMGOLD, the DCF here uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The model starts from last twelve months free cash flow of about $126.8 million and uses analyst projections for several years, then extends those further using Simply Wall St estimates. For example, projected free cash flow for 2030 is $975.4 million, with intermediate years such as 2026 to 2029 ranging between about $1.1 billion and $1.7 billion before discounting, according to the model data.

Bringing these projected cash flows back to today results in an estimated intrinsic value of $40.17 per share. Compared with the recent share price of C$27.69, this DCF output implies the stock is about 31.1% undervalued on this measure.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests IAMGOLD is undervalued by 31.1%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 7 more high quality undervalued stocks.IMG Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for IAMGOLD.

Approach 2: IAMGOLD Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about value because it ties the share price directly to the earnings that support it. A higher or lower P/E often reflects what the market is willing to pay for each dollar of earnings, given expectations for growth and the level of risk.

Story Continues

In general, companies with stronger expected earnings growth and lower perceived risk tend to justify a higher “normal” P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually lines up with a lower P/E. IAMGOLD trades on a P/E of 17.54x, compared with an average of 18.74x for the Metals and Mining industry and about 22.86x for its peer group.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for IAMGOLD is 18.26x. This Fair Ratio is a proprietary view of what the P/E “should” be, based on factors like earnings growth, profit margin, industry, market cap and company specific risks. That makes it more tailored than simple comparisons with peers or the broad industry, which do not adjust for these differences. With a current P/E of 17.54x versus a Fair Ratio of 18.26x, the shares screen as slightly undervalued on this measure.

Result: UNDERVALUEDTSX:IMG P/E Ratio as at Apr 2026

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 2 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your IAMGOLD Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Meet Narratives, a simple tool on Simply Wall St’s Community page that lets you set a clear story for IAMGOLD, link that story to specific forecasts for revenue, earnings and margins, and see the Fair Value that falls out of those assumptions. You can then compare it with the current share price to help decide whether IAMGOLD looks more like a buy, a hold, or a sell for you. The numbers update automatically as fresh information such as news, production guidance or earnings arrives. This means that a more optimistic view, such as a Fair Value around CA$40.80 with revenue growing at 9.0% a year and margins rising to 29.4%, can sit side by side with a more cautious view, such as a Fair Value around CA$11.04 with earnings of about $436.1m and a 17.5% margin, and you can see exactly which story you find more convincing.

title: IAMGOLD Provides Notice of First Quarter 2026 Results and Conference Call
content:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - April 10, 2026) - IAMGOLD Corporation (TSX: IMG) (NYSE: IAG) ("IAMGOLD" or the "Company") is pleased to announce it plans to release its first quarter 2026 operating and financial results after market hours on Tuesday, May 5, 2026. Senior management will host a conference call to discuss the operating performance and financial results on Wednesday, May 6, 2026, at 8:30 a.m.(Eastern Time).

Listeners may access the conference call via webcast from the events section of the Company's website at www.iamgold.com (webcast link below), or through the following options:

Pre-register via: Chorus Call IAMGOLD Q2 2026 Registration(recommended) – Upon registering, you will receive a calendar booking by email with dial-in details and unique PIN. This process will bypass the operator and avoid the queue.
Toll free (North America): 1 (833) 752-3518
International: 1 (647) 846-8209
Webcast: https://event.choruscall.com/mediaframe/webcast.html?webcastid=l2jB5vCu

An online archive of the webcast will be available by accessing the Company's website at www.iamgold.com. A telephone replay will be available for one month following the call by dialing toll free 1 (855) 669-9658 within North America or 1 (412) 317-0088 from international locations and entering the passcode: 2979130.

About IAMGOLD

IAMGOLD is an intermediate gold producer and developer based in Canada with operating mines in North America and West Africa, including Côté Gold (Canada), Westwood (Canada) and Essakane (Burkina Faso). The Côté Gold Mine is among the largest gold mines in production in Canada, which IAMGOLD operates in a 70|30 partnership with Sumitomo Metal Mining Co. Ltd. ("SMM"). In addition, the Company has an established portfolio of early stage and advanced exploration projects within high potential mining districts, including the large-scale Nelligan Mining Complex located in Quebec, Canada. IAMGOLD employs approximately 3,700 people and is committed to maintaining its culture of accountable mining through high standards of Environmental, Social and Governance practices. IAMGOLD is listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: IAG) and the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX: IMG).

IAMGOLD Contact Information

Graeme Jennings, Vice President, Business Development & Investor Relations
Tel: 416 360 4743 | Mobile: 416 388 6883
Toll-free: 1 888 464 9999
info@iamgold.com

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/292036

title: Why IAMGOLD (TSX:IMG) Is Up 6.6% After Joining The FTSE All-World Index And What's Next
content:
In March 2026, IAMGOLD Corporation (TSX: IMG) was added to the FTSE All-World Index (USD), marking its inclusion in a widely followed global benchmark. This index inclusion can expand IAMGOLD’s potential investor base by drawing interest from global equity funds and index-tracking products that reference the FTSE All-World universe. Next, we’ll examine how IAMGOLD’s addition to the FTSE All-World Index could influence its investment narrative and institutional appeal.

Invest in the nuclear renaissance through our list of 89 elite nuclear energy infrastructure plays powering the global AI revolution.

IAMGOLD Investment Narrative Recap

To own IAMGOLD, you need to believe the company can manage costs and execution risk at core assets like Côté and Essakane while maintaining healthy margins despite its sizable net debt. The FTSE All-World Index inclusion mainly strengthens IAMGOLD’s visibility and potential liquidity; it does not materially change the near term operational execution risk or cost pressures that remain central to the story.

The index addition sits alongside IAMGOLD’s recently completed repurchase of 3,000,000 shares for about C$50 million, the first tranche of a much larger buyback program. That capital return initiative, funded after debt reduction, has been part of the company’s appeal for investors watching production trends and cost control. Together, the buyback and index inclusion sharpen attention on how sustainably IAMGOLD can turn its current production base into ongoing cash generation.

Yet beneath this broader interest, investors should be aware that cost pressures at key assets could still...

title: Is It Time To Reassess IAMGOLD (TSX:IMG) After The Recent Share Price Pullback
content:
Find winning stocks in any market cycle. Join 7 million investors using Simply Wall St's investing ideas for FREE.

If you are wondering whether IAMGOLD’s current share price reflects its true worth, the recent moves in the stock raise some important valuation questions to consider. The share price closed at C$22.10, with a 7 day return of an 18.8% decline and a 30 day return of a 24.2% decline, while the 1 year return stands at 155.5% and the 5 year return is 477.0%. These sharp short term moves sit alongside very large 3 year gains. This helps explain why IAMGOLD has been on the radar of more investors lately. Recent commentary has focused on how this longer term rerating compares with the pullback over the past month and what that means for risk and reward at today’s price. On Simply Wall St’s valuation checks, IAMGOLD scores 6 out of 6 for being assessed as undervalued. The rest of this article will unpack how different valuation methods arrive at that view, before finishing with a more holistic way to think about what the market might be pricing in.

IAMGOLD delivered 155.5% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Metals and Mining industry.

Approach 1: IAMGOLD Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model looks at the cash IAMGOLD is expected to generate in the future, then discounts those cash flows back to today to estimate what the business might be worth in total.

IAMGOLD’s latest twelve month free cash flow is $126.8 million. Analysts provide explicit free cash flow estimates for the next few years, and Simply Wall St then extends those projections out to 2035 using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model. Within those projections, free cash flow in 2030 is estimated at $975.4 million, with intermediate years such as 2026 and 2027 projected at $1,677.3 million and $1,730.0 million respectively, all before discounting.

After discounting these projected cash flows back to today, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of $41.16 per share. Compared to the recent share price of CA$22.10, the DCF output implies the stock trades at a 46.3% discount. This points to IAMGOLD being assessed as materially undervalued on this model alone.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests IAMGOLD is undervalued by 46.3%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 4 more high quality undervalued stocks.IMG Discounted Cash Flow as at Mar 2026

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for IAMGOLD.

Approach 2: IAMGOLD Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful way to relate what you pay for the stock to the earnings the business is currently generating. It gives you a quick sense of how many years of earnings the market is effectively pricing in at today’s share price.

Story Continues

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how the market views growth potential and risk. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk usually support a higher P/E, while slower expected growth or higher risk tend to justify a lower multiple.

IAMGOLD currently trades on a P/E of 14.28x. That sits below the Metals and Mining industry average P/E of 16.32x and below the peer group average of 21.13x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 23.57x for IAMGOLD, which reflects factors such as its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margin, market cap and company specific risks.

This Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the broad industry, because it adjusts for the characteristics of IAMGOLD itself rather than assuming all miners should trade on the same multiple. Comparing the current 14.28x P/E to the 23.57x Fair Ratio suggests the shares are priced below that customised benchmark.

Result: UNDERVALUEDTSX:IMG P/E Ratio as at Mar 2026

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 3 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your IAMGOLD Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives on Simply Wall St give you a clear story behind the numbers by letting you connect your view on IAMGOLD’s future revenue, earnings and margins to a specific forecast and Fair Value, then compare that to today’s price to help decide whether the stock looks expensive or cheap based on your own assumptions.

On the Community page you can see different Narratives side by side. For example, there is a more optimistic IAMGOLD view that ties a Fair Value of about CA$36.55 to assumptions like higher long term profit margins and a future P/E of 21.67x. There is also a more cautious view that links a Fair Value closer to CA$11.04 to expectations of thinner margins and a future P/E of 14.6x. Because these Narratives update automatically when new results, guidance or news are added, your valuation stays aligned with the latest information rather than a static one off model.
